message:
feat(render,runtime): add configurable degradation floor to prevent content-suppressing quality levels

Add a degradation_floor field to BudgetControllerConfig, CascadeConfig,
and CascadePolicyConfig that establishes a minimum quality level below
which the system will never degrade, regardless of frame budget pressure.

Problem: Transient focus/resize spikes or brief frame budget overruns
could cause the e-process budget controller and degradation cascade to
escalate through EssentialOnly → Skeleton → SkipFrame, suppressing
readable text content even when the spike is momentary.

Solution: The degradation floor (default: SimpleBorders) acts as a
safety net independent of max_degradation. When the controller or
cascade attempts to degrade past the floor, the level is clamped:

Budget controller (ftui-render/budget.rs):
- Check degradation_floor alongside is_max() before deciding to
  degrade — skip degradation if already at floor
- Clamp next level to floor after transition to prevent overshoot

Degradation cascade (ftui-runtime/degradation_cascade.rs):
- Add degradation_floor condition to cascade degrade path
- Post-transition clamp ensures min_trigger_level jump doesn't
  bypass the floor

Policy config (ftui-runtime/policy_config.rs):
- Add degradation_floor to CascadePolicyConfig with serde support
- Propagate through to_cascade_config()
- Add assertion in existing policy round-trip test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
